title: Simulating tracks with the availability package
text: package provides methods estimating geographic space available animal, based observed track collected GPS tag similar. Broadly, functions generate simulated track preserves certain properties original track. simulated tracks can optionally constrained land mask (mask prevents simulated track visiting unwanted locations). can also forced pass fixed points certain times, allowing (example) simulated tracks finish point started. simulated tracks attempt preserve basic properties observed track, speeds exhibited animal overall track duration. intention explicitly reproduce behavioural states area-restricted searching. Rather, goal estimate plausible track animal might produced, environmental preferences governing movements. geographic environmental properties actual track can compared simulated tracks, giving insights preferred habitats animal. application examples, see e.g. Raymond et al. (2015), Reisinger et al. (2018), Courbin et al. (2018), Péron et al. (2018).

title: Simulating tracks with the availability package
text: Load availability package, others ’ll use: helper functions used demo:
code: library(availability) library(trip) library(aniMotum) library(geosphere) library(ggplot2) library(maps) cpal <- c("#1B9E77", "#D95F02", "#7570B3", "#E7298A", "#66A61E", "#E6AB02") base_map <- function(lon_range, lat_range) {     ggplot(mapping = aes(lon, lat)) +         borders("world", fill="grey90",colour="grey") +         coord_cartesian(xlim = lon_range + diff(lon_range) * 0.05 * c(-1, 1),                         ylim = lat_range + diff(lat_range) * 0.05 * c(-1, 1)) +         theme_bw() }

title: Simulating tracks with the availability package
text: track sooty albatross Prince Edwards Islands, Reisinger et al. (2018). First pass data aniMotum’s fit_ssm function produce filtered track evenly-sampled 3-hourly time steps: Plot raw (magenta) filtered/interpolated (green) versions track:
code: data(ptt_data) time_step <- 3 set.seed(28)  track_data <- ptt_data$DMS[, c("individual_id", "date", "location_quality",                                "decimal_longitude", "decimal_latitude")] names(track_data) <- c("id", "date", "lc", "lon", "lat") fit <- fit_ssm(track_data, time.step = time_step, control = ssm_control(verbose = 0)) pred <- grab(fit, what = "predicted") base_map(range(track_data$lon), range(track_data$lat)) +     geom_path(data = track_data, colour = cpal[4]) + ## observed track     geom_path(data = pred, colour = cpal[1]) ## filtered track

what: Simulating a track by the VAR method
title: Simulating tracks with the availability package
text: Fit vector-autoregressive movement model filtered track. model assumes x- y-speeds time t linear function speeds time t-1, plus random noise. Now can use fitted model generate new tracks. default, end points simulated track fixed start end points original track, land mask applied: Plot observed (filtered/interpolated) track (green), simulated track (orange), track end points (red dots).
code: arfit <- surrogateARModel(pred[, c("lon", "lat")]) trkar <- surrogateAR(arfit, as.matrix(pred[, c("lon", "lat")]), pred$date) sx <- setNames(as.data.frame(trkar$xs), c("lon", "lat")) ## simulated track as a data.frame  base_map(range(track_data$lon, sx$lon), range(track_data$lat, sx$lat)) +     geom_path(data = pred, colour = cpal[1]) + ## observed track     geom_path(data = sx, colour = cpal[2]) + ## simulated track     geom_point(data = pred[c(1, nrow(pred)), ], col = "red") ## start and end points

what: Comparison of simulated tracks to the observed track
title: Simulating tracks with the availability package
text: speed distributions tracks can compared directly:  observed track appears number segments slow speeds (see peak histogram around 3 km/h, possibly related area-restricted searching), superimposed broad distribution speeds 35 km/h. AR-based method fails reproduce quite shape distribution — peaks higher speed — produce simulated track plausible sense speeds largely range observed track.
code: step_len <- function(z) distVincentyEllipsoid(z[-nrow(z), 1:2], z[-1, 1:2]) / 1e3 temp <- data.frame(speed = step_len(pred[, c("lon", "lat")]) / time_step, track = "Observed") temp <- rbind(temp, data.frame(speed = step_len(trkar$xs) / time_step, track = "AR")) ggplot(data = temp, aes(x = speed, fill = track)) + geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.5) +     facet_grid(track~.) + scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(from = 0, to = 60, by = 5)) +     xlab("Speed (km/h)") + scale_fill_manual(values = cpal) + xlim(c(0, 60))

title: Simulating tracks with the availability package
text: default, end points simulated track fixed start end points original track, land mask applied. land mask can applied via point.check argument. example, using land mask supplied package:  point.check argument accepts function form function(tm, pt) returns TRUE point accepted FALSE . Note function can accept time coordinate well location, mask can made time-varying required (e.g. dynamically masking areas covered sea ice).
code: trkar <- surrogateAR(arfit, as.matrix(pred[, c("lon", "lat")]), pred$date,                      point.check = gshhsMask())  sx <- setNames(as.data.frame(trkar$xs), c("lon", "lat")) ## simulated track as a data.frame base_map(range(track_data$lon, sx$lon), range(track_data$lat, sx$lat)) +     geom_path(data = grab(fit, what = "predicted"), colour = cpal[1]) + ## observed track     geom_path(data = sx, colour = cpal[2]) + ## simulated track     geom_point(data = pred[c(1, nrow(pred)), ], col = "red") ## start and end points

title: Simulating tracks with the availability package
text: default, first last points simulated track fixed match template track. fixed points can removed, fixed points enforced, via fixed parameter. (AR method, first point must always fixed.) Fix additional arbitrary point path (indicated blue point plot):  Repeat fix final endpoint:
code: fixed <- logical(nrow(pred)) fixed[1] <- fixed[nrow(pred)] <- fixed[750] <- TRUE ## fix the first, last, and 750th point  trkar <- surrogateAR(arfit, as.matrix(pred[, c("lon", "lat")]), pred$date, fixed,                      point.check = gshhsMask()) sx <- setNames(as.data.frame(trkar$xs), c("lon", "lat")) ## simulated track as a data.frame base_map(range(track_data$lon, sx$lon), range(track_data$lat, sx$lat)) +     geom_path(data = grab(fit, what = "predicted"), colour = cpal[1]) + ## observed track     geom_path(data = sx, colour = cpal[2]) + ## simulated track     geom_point(data = pred[c(1, nrow(pred)), ], col = "red") + ## start and end points     geom_point(data = sx[750, ], col = "blue") ## fixed point fixed <- logical(nrow(pred)) fixed[1] <- fixed[750] <- TRUE ## fix the first and 750th point trkar <- surrogateAR(arfit, as.matrix(pred[, c("lon", "lat")]), pred$date, fixed,                      point.check = gshhsMask()) sx <- setNames(as.data.frame(trkar$xs), c("lon", "lat")) ## simulated track as a data.frame base_map(range(track_data$lon, sx$lon), range(track_data$lat, sx$lat)) +     geom_path(data = grab(fit, what = "predicted"), colour = cpal[1]) + ## observed track     geom_path(data = sx, colour = cpal[2]) + ## simulated track     geom_point(data = pred[c(1, nrow(pred)), ], col = "red") + ## start and end points     geom_point(data = sx[750, ], col = "blue") ## fixed point

title: Simulating tracks with the availability package
text: Elephant seal data Reisinger et al. (2018). Use fitted model determine parameters generate new path fixing ends, using land mask: Compare tracks (original track green, AR-simulated track orange):  Compare speed distributions :
code: track_data <- ptt_data$SES[, c("individual_id", "date", "location_quality",                                "decimal_longitude", "decimal_latitude")] names(track_data) <- c("id", "date", "lc", "lon", "lat") fit <- fit_ssm(track_data, time.step = time_step, control = ssm_control(verbose = 0)) pred <- grab(fit, what = "predicted") arfit <- surrogateARModel(pred[, c("lon", "lat")]) trkar <- surrogateAR(arfit, as.matrix(pred[, c("lon", "lat")]), pred$date,                      point.check = gshhsMask()) sx <- setNames(as.data.frame(trkar$xs), c("lon", "lat")) ## simulated track as a data.frame base_map(range(track_data$lon, sx$lon), range(track_data$lat, sx$lat)) +     geom_path(data = grab(fit, what = "predicted"), colour = cpal[1]) + ## observed track     geom_path(data = sx, colour = cpal[2]) + ## simulated track     geom_point(data = pred[c(1, nrow(pred)), ], col = "red") ## start and end points temp <- data.frame(speed = step_len(pred[, c("lon", "lat")]) / time_step, track = "Observed") temp <- rbind(temp, data.frame(speed = step_len(trkar$xs) / time_step, track = "AR")) ggplot(data = temp, aes(x = speed, fill = track)) + geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.5) +     facet_grid(track~.) + scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(from = 0, to = 20, by = 5)) +     xlab("Speed (km/h)") + scale_fill_manual(values = cpal) + xlim(c(0, 20))

title: Simulating tracks with the availability package
text: [1] Raymond B, Lea MA, Patterson T, Andrews-Goff V, Sharples R, Charrassin J-B, Cottin M, Emmerson L, Gales N, Gales R, Goldsworthy SD, Harcourt R, Kato , Kirkwood R, Lawton K, Ropert-Coudert Y, Southwell C, van den Hoff J, Wienecke B, Woehler EJ, Wotherspoon S, Hindell MA (2015) Important marine habitat East Antarctica revealed two decades multi-species predator tracking. Ecography. doi:10.1111/ecog.01021 [2] Reisinger RR, Raymond B, Hindell MA, Bester MN, Crawford RJM, Davies D, de Bruyn PJN, Dilley BJ, Kirkman SP, Makhado AB, Ryan PG, Schoombie S, Stevens K, Sumner MD, Tosh CA, Wege M, Whitehead , Wotherspoon S, Pistorius PA (2018) Habitat modelling tracking data multiple marine top predators reveals important habitat Southern Indian Ocean. Diversity Distributions. doi:10.1111/ddi.12702 [3] Courbin N, Besnard , Péron C, Saraux C, Fort J, Perret S, Tornos J, Grémillet D (2018) Short-term prey field lability constrains individual specialisation resource selection foraging site fidelity marine predator. Ecology Letters. doi:10.1111/ele.12970 [4] Péron C, Authier M, Grémillet D (2018) Testing transferability track-based habitat models sound marine spatial planning. Diversity Distributions. doi:10.1111/ddi.12832

what: Estimating Geographic Space Available to Animals Based on Telemetry Data
title: Estimating Geographic Space Available to Animals Based on Telemetry Data
text: R package estimating geographic space available animals based telemetry data.

title: Estimating Geographic Space Available to Animals Based on Telemetry Data
code: library(remotes) install_github("AustralianAntarcticDivision/availability")

title: Estimating Geographic Space Available to Animals Based on Telemetry Data
text: Using vector-AR method (see [1]): using crawl-based track simulator:
code: library(availability) ## load observed track, 2-column matrix of longitude and latitude ## the track points should be equally sampled in time realtrack <- ... ## your data here  arf <- surrogateARModel(realtrack) ## fit AR model to track  st <- surrogateAR(arf, realtrack) ## simulate new track library(crawl)  ## fit a crawl model to your raw track data fit <- crwMLE(...)  ## regularly-spaced times for which you want positions time_step <- 3 ## e.g. using a time step of 3 hours predTime <- seq(my_starting_date, my_ending_date, by = time_step*3600)  ## extract predicted positions at those times predObj <- crwPredict(fit, predTime = predTime, speedEst = TRUE, flat = TRUE)  ## keep only regularly-interpolated locations pr <- data.frame(date = predTime, predObj[predObj$locType == "p", ])  ## construct the corresponding transition and covariance matrices of the ##  state space model model <- surrogateCrawlModel(fit, time_step)  ## and finally simulate the track stcrw <- surrogateCrawl(model, as.matrix(pr[, c("mu.x", "mu.y", "nu.x", "nu.y")]), pr$date)

title: Estimating Geographic Space Available to Animals Based on Telemetry Data
text: Please note: crawl-based functions, package developed version 1 crawl package. also work crawl v2, note v2 works projected coordinates (longitude latitude).

title: Land Mask — etopoMask
text: land mask based full ETOPO1 dataset

title: Land Mask — etopoMask
code: etopoMask(   basename = "ETOPO1_Bed_c_geotiff",   path = ".",   tmp = path,   land = FALSE )

title: Land Mask — etopoMask
text: basename name etopo geotiff (without file extension). path path folder containing etopo geotiff tmp path writeable folder land logical value return land.

title: Land Mask — etopoMask
text: logical indicating whether point land sea.

title: Land Mask — etopoMask
text: Generate land mask function based ETOPO1 topography. etopo geotiff bundled package must downloaded https://www.ngdc.noaa.gov/mgg/global/global.html. mask intially created, native raster (grd, gri) version geotiff created directory tmp, must writable. file can deleted computation finished. land argument determines whether mask function returns TRUE FALSE land. mask constant tm argument mask ignored.

what: A land mask based on the GSHHS data set — gshhsMask
title: A land mask based on the GSHHS data set — gshhsMask
text: Generate land mask function based Global Self-consistent, Hierarchical, High-resolution Geography Database. mask provided two (approximate) spatial resolutions: 0.1 degree 0.05 degrees. latter requires significantly memory. mask constant tm argument mask ignored.

title: A land mask based on the GSHHS data set — gshhsMask
code: gshhsMask(res = 0.1, latmin = -90, latmax = 90)  landmask_init(res = 0.1, latmin = -90, latmax = 90)

title: A land mask based on the GSHHS data set — gshhsMask
text: res numeric: spatial resolution mask, degrees (either 0.1 0.05) latmin numeric: southernmost latitude extent land mask latmax numeric: northernmost latitude extent land mask

title: A land mask based on the GSHHS data set — gshhsMask
text: function returns logical indicating whether point sea (TRUE) land (FALSE)

title: A land mask based on the GSHHS data set — gshhsMask
text: Wessel P, Smith WHF (1996) Global Self-consistent, Hierarchical, High-resolution Shoreline Database. J. Geophys. Res. 101: 8741-8743. https://www.ngdc.noaa.gov/mgg/shorelines/gshhs.html

title: A land mask based on the GSHHS data set — gshhsMask
code: mask  <- gshhsMask() ## initialize land mask function mask(0, c(100, -65)) ## test point lon,lat #> [1] TRUE

title: VAR bridge sampler — surrogateAR
text: Generate new tracks VAR(1)

title: VAR bridge sampler — surrogateAR
code: surrogateAR(   model,   xs,   ts = seq_len(nrow(xs)),   fixed = rep(c(TRUE, FALSE, TRUE), c(1, nrow(xs) - 2, 1)),   point.check = function(tm, pt) TRUE,   random.rotation = c(-pi, pi),   partial = FALSE )

title: VAR bridge sampler — surrogateAR
text: model model generated surrogateARModel. xs template sequence states ts times track sampled fixed logical vector indicating locations template path held fixed. point.check function accepts state returns boolean indicating whether state acceptable. random.rotation upper lower limits (radians) rotation applied VAR(1) model. partial TRUE, partial track returned sampling fails.

title: VAR bridge sampler — surrogateAR
text: array states define simulated path.

title: VAR bridge sampler — surrogateAR
text: Given fitted VAR(1) model template track, function generates new track length coincides template start point optionally specified points along track. template track must supplied matrix representing sequence locations row location columns represent longitude latitude.  locations must equispaced time, can generated "p" location types crwPredict. model object generated surrogateARModel filtered, iunterpolated track.  fits VAR(1) model increments longitude latitude. Locations template track can marked fixed fixed argument. current implementation first location must always fixed. Additional constraints can placed path rejection sampling function point.check.  function must accept state return boolean indicating whether point acceptable.  example, track can constrained ocean supplying point.check function compares state land mask returns FALSE states corresponding locations fall land.

Verr error covariance fixed points. partial TRUE, partial track returned sampling fails.

title: Regressive bridge sampler — surrogateCrawl

title: Regressive bridge sampler — surrogateCrawl
text: Given fitted crawl model template track, function generates new track length coincides template start point optionally specified points along track. template track must supplied matrix representing sequence states row state column state variable.  states must equispaced time, can generated "p" location types crwPredict. crawl model object generated crawlModel fitted crawl model. Locations template track can marked fixed fixed argument. current implementation first location must always fixed. Additional constraints can placed path rejection sampling function point.check.  function must accept state return boolean indicating whether point acceptable.  example, track can constrained ocean supplying point.check function compares state land mask returns FALSE states corresponding locations fall land.

title: Crawl Movement Model — surrogateCrawlModel
text: Generates transition covariance matrices state space model time increment dt, corresponding fitted crawl model drift covariates. Currently polar adjustment made. matrices generated assuming state variables stored vector order longitude mu nu latitude mu nu.  parametrization defined crawl source paper, differs factor \(\beta^{2}\) definition \(\sigma^{2}\).

title: Simulated VAR(1) tracks — surrogate_arsimulate
text: Simulate track fitted vector autoregressive model

title: Simulated VAR(1) tracks — surrogate_arsimulate
code: surrogate_arsimulate(   arfit,   n,   startlonlat,   fixed = NULL,   endlonlat = NULL,   do.test.land = TRUE,   random.rotation = c(-pi, pi),   verbose = 0,   return.all.points = FALSE,   intermediate.tries = 10,   original = FALSE )  surrogate_arsimulate0(   arfit,   n,   startlonlat,   fixed = NULL,   endlonlat = NULL,   do.test.land = TRUE,   random.rotation = c(-pi, pi),   verbose = 0,   return.all.points = FALSE,   intermediate.tries = 10 )

title: Simulated VAR(1) tracks — surrogate_arsimulate
text: arfit fitted object class "ar" returned surrogate_arfit n number points simulate startlonlat 2-element vector starting longitude latitude fixed dataframe matrix first column index (1:n) fixed point, second third columns give associated longitude latitude endlonlat 2-element vector ending longitude latitude. NULL, end constraint imposed except land masking (land masking used). simple way imposing return--starting-location constraint; complex constraints use fixed argument .test.land logical function. TRUE, use included land mask avoid land. Alternatively, function can passed returns TRUE (point okay, land) FALSE (point land) given lon,lat. Note land masking ignored fixed points. Note also possible create sitation tracks difficult impossible simulate, fixed point sufficiently far onto land track reach . random.rotation 2-element vector giving range rotation apply randomized track (values radians). use random.rotation=NULL rotation. angle can restricted using random.rotation=c(min.angle,max.angle) - may speed computation avoiding impossible angles (e.g. tracks land mass) verbose integer 0-3, >0 spit extra information may helpful things work expected. Larger numbers mean output return..points TRUE, return points proposed rejected due land masking (may helpful debugging). TRUE, returned data.frame extra column named "valid" intermediate.tries land-masking, try many times find valid point step giving starting ? Higher values may improve overall run-time, -high values may yield tracks good representation fitted model original TRUE, use original algorithm.

title: Simulated VAR(1) tracks — surrogate_arsimulate
text: 2 3 column dataframe longitude latitude simulated track points (point validity, return..points TRUE)
